description_long: This is an ancient-style bronze kettle with a distinctive tripod base, featuring three elongated, pointed legs that provide stability. The body is cylindrical and slightly tapered, with a wide, flared mouth that forms a spout on one side for pouring. A prominent, side-mounted handle arches from the upper body to the midsection, allowing for easy gripping and maneuvering. The top of the kettle is adorned with two conical, knob-like protrusions, one of which likely serves as a lid or cover, while the other may be decorative or functional. The surface has a weathered, matte patina, characteristic of aged bronze, and appears plain without any visible decorative patterns. This kettle's robust construction and utilitarian design suggest it was intended for practical use, possibly for heating or serving liquids.
description: A weathered bronze tripod kettle with a flared spout, side handle, and two conical knobs on top, featuring a plain, matte surface.
description_view_0: Front view shows the wide flared spout, both conical knobs, and the full tripod leg arrangement.
description_view_1: This angled view highlights the curvature of the spout and the side handle, with the legs partially overlapping.
description_view_2: Side view emphasizes the length of the spout and the alignment of the two knobs, with the handle less visible.
description_view_3: Rear view displays the side-mounted handle prominently and the back curvature of the kettle, with the spout less visible.
